Dear Mr. Sutton:
we have received your letter dated July 9, L99o, seeking

29, L99o. That advice related to your question concerning whether
the State Air Resources Board's review of south coast Air Quality
Management District air quality guidelines constitutes administra-
tive action under Section 82039 of the Political Refom Act (the

17 , L99Q, we confirm that your tetter reflects the advice provided
to you.2
on the basis of the facts your provided, you were advised
that the state Air Resources Board review of the South coast Air

action.
our advice was premised on the following analysis. 
The
lobbyist registration and reporting requirements contained in
chapter 6 of the Act (Sections 86100 et seq. ) apply to individuals
lcovernment Code Sections 81OOO-91015. Al-t statutory references
are to the covernment code unless otherwise indicated.

references to regulations are to Title 2, Division 5 of the
CaLifornia code of Regulations.
)' 
Because you have not provided the name of your client, we are
treating your request for confinnation of advice to be for
infornal assistance onIy. Informal assistance does not provide
the reguestor with the irnnunity provided by an opinion or formal
written advice. (Section 83114; Regulation 18329(c) (3), copy

428 J Street, Suite 8OO o P.O. Box 8O7 o Sacramento CA 958O4-O8O7 . (9f6)322-566O

and their employers who communicate rrith an agency official 
for
the purpose of influencing legislative or administrative action.
(section a2O39. ) 
ttAgency officialtr means any member, officer,
employee or consultant of any state agency. -(Section a2oo4.) The
State Air Resources Board is a state agency under sectiol] 82049.
The south Coast Air Quality lIanagement District is not a state
agency. (Lohrell Advice Letter, No. A-84-291, copy enclosed.)
rrAdministrative actiontr toeans the proposal, drafting,
developrnent, consideration, amendment, enactment or defeat by any
state agency of any ru1e, regulation or other action in any rate-
rnaking proceeding or any quas i-legislative 
proceeding. (Section
a2oo2. ) Therefore, a lobbyist provides lobbying services when he
or she represents a client in a quasi-J.egis1ative proceeding
before an administrative agency.
The Cornmission bases its determination of whether a proceed-
ing before a public agency is quasi-legislative or quasi-judicial
on relevant case law. (See fn re curiel (1983) 8 FPPC ops. 1, In
re Evans (1978) 4 FPPC Ops. 84; In re Leonard (1976) 2 FPPC Ops.
54i fn re Carson (1975) 1 FPPC Ops. 46, copies encl,osed. ) 
The
State Air Resources Board's review of the Bay Area Air Quality
ltanagement District has been hetd to be a quasi- legislative
proceeding in Stauffer ChenicaL Co. v. Air Resources Board ((1982)
128 Cal .App.3d 789, 793, LAO Cal.Rptr. 550). In Stauffer, the
state board pursuant to statute reviewed the 1ocal district's
regulations and adopted amendments to the local district's 
regula-
tions after notice and a public hearing. Here, as in Stauffer,
the state board is reviewing the loca1 district's 
plan by giving
notice and conducting a public hearing. (section 4L5o2.)
Therefore, the state board proceeding is quasi-legislative and
t'administrative action" for purposes of the Act.
I trust this letter has provided you with the guidance you
requested. If you have any further questions regarding this nat-
ter, please contact ne at (916) 322-59OL.
Sincerely,
Scott

sections.question: Whether the State Air Resources Board's review of South Coast Air Quality Management District air quality guidelines constitutes administrative action under Section 82039 of the Political Reform Act.
sections.conclusion: On the basis of the facts you provided, you were advised that the State Air Resources Board review of the South Coast Air Quality Management District guidelines constituted administrative action. The State board proceeding is quasi-legislative and 'administrative action' for purposes of the Act.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether the State Air Resources Board's review of local air quality guidelines constitutes administrative action triggering lobbyist registration and reporting requirements under the Political Reform Act.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC confirmed that the State Air Resources Board's review of local air quality guidelines constitutes administrative action under the Political Reform Act, as it is a quasi-legislative proceeding involving the proposal, amendment, and potential adoption of rules affecting a local district's regulations.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Informal advice letter confirming telephone advice; requestor did not provide client name, limiting protections; multiple case law references and statutory citations provided to support quasi-legislative determination; includes regulatory references to Title 2, Division 5.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether the State Air Resources Board's review of South Coast Air Quality Management District air quality guidelines constitutes administrative action under Section 82039 of the Political Reform Act.

CONCLUSION: On the basis of the facts you provided, you were advised that the State Air Resources Board review of the South Coast Air Quality Management District guidelines constituted administrative action. The State board proceeding is quasi-legislative and 'administrative action' for purposes of the Act.

embedding.summary: FPPC written confirmation of prior telephone advice that the State Air Resources Board's review of South Coast Air Quality Management District air quality guidelines constitutes administrative action under Section 82039, triggering lobbyist registration and reporting requirements.
